name: Red Sea
shortDescription: The Red Sea is a sea between Africa and Asia, most famously known in Jewish mythology as the body of water miraculously parted by Moses to allow the Israelites to escape from the pursuing Egyptian army.
description: The Red Sea holds immense significance in Jewish mythology, primarily due to the Exodus narrative. According to the Book of Exodus, the Israelites, led by Moses, were fleeing slavery in Egypt under the rule of Pharaoh. As they reached the shores of the Red Sea, they found themselves trapped between the water and the approaching Egyptian army. In a divine intervention, Moses, empowered by God and wielding the staff of Moses, stretched out his hand over the sea. God then caused a strong east wind to blow all night, dividing the waters, and creating a dry path for the Israelites to cross. The Israelites traversed the sea on dry ground, with walls of water on their right and left. Once the Israelites had safely crossed, the Egyptians pursued them into the sea. However, God caused the wheels of their chariots to become stuck, and as the last of the Israelites reached the other side, Moses stretched out his hand again, and the waters returned, engulfing the entire Egyptian army, ensuring the Israelites' freedom. This event is a foundational myth in Judaism, symbolizing deliverance, divine power, and the covenant between God and the Israelites. The Red Sea, therefore, represents both a physical barrier and a site of miraculous salvation.

location.cosmological: A sea located between Africa and Asia, historically a significant trade route, and mythologically a site of divine intervention.

sources.title: Ancient Israel: From Abraham to the Roman Destruction of the Temple
sources.author: Hershel Shanks
